Read the spec — Workout.md is a minimal-friction, tracking-first workout tracker where Markdown is the storage layer, deviations are first-class, and the AI coach is a configurable policy layer that repairs plans forward. Asked what to do next.
how do you imagine the app? high level

Described a high-level vision: one screen you live in during a workout (Today), with planning/calendar and coach settings tucked behind it. AI is invisible by default — ghost targets, inline nudges, a single "what should I do?" action — and outputs plan diffs, not chatter. Offered to build an HTML mockup of the Today screen.
make a wireframe runnable prototype use the multiple skills I added for you: ios-hig-design, mobile-ios-design, liquid-glass-design run it on my computere asap use sonnet agents to do the coding
Noted that only liquid-glass-design is installed; applied iOS HIG principles directly and loaded the liquid-glass skill. Handed the build to a Sonnet agent.

Prototype is open in the browser — a single-file HTML app with CSS Liquid Glass treatment, three working tabs (Today, Plan, Coach), ghost-vs-actual set logging, coach nudges, and a real Export .md button. Offered to tune interactions, build a native SwiftUI version, or iterate.

no, build it as an actual ios app -- I have xcode and everything! don't use card containers -- those are for the web and look bad on iphone -- bleed-edge instead. I do circuits or supersets very often so make that representable on the UI. the UI should be tik-tok like where I only see what set I need to do next and perhaps cues or something that can be attached by the coach for the exercise. I should be able to tap next or provide very easily feedback on how easy/hard it felt or write a note or whatever. open it on my ios simulator when ready
Launched the native iOS app on the simulator and started capturing screenshots to verify rendering.
Tapped Start to enter the runner.

Confirmed the runner looks correct — full-bleed, floating glass top strip, giant hero target, coach cue pill, effort pills, reps stepper, and glass control cluster. Advanced into the superset block to check the circuit mini-map.
Caught a mid-scroll frame but confirmed the superset page shows round indicator, mini-map, purple block hue, and its own coach cue. Grabbed a clean settled frame next.
Applied a fix for page ghosting — pages were shorter than the screen due to safeAreaInset. Rebuilding and reinstalling.
Ghosting is gone — clean single full-screen pages with opaque edge-to-edge backgrounds. Confirmed a superset page is equally clean.

App is running clean on the simulator. Summarized what's built: a real native SwiftUI iOS 26 app with Liquid Glass APIs, TikTok-style runner with one set per full-screen page, first-class superset/circuit support, coach cues, effort/skip/note feedback, and a fix for the page ghosting issue. Noted the reps stepper spacing is tight and the Done summary + circuit block haven't been screenshotted yet.

yeah, polish -- the spacing feels off -- controls are also too far from the top and from the bottom of the screen. For stuff that's time-based (like a plank) I should be able to start the timer and see the countdown. Also, give me some way, maybe tapping the title thing at the top, to see the full list. Also, the reps number and - + buttons should be bigger

Queued all four fixes: rebalance control spacing, add a countdown timer for timed sets with a depleting ring, make the top pill open a full-list sheet, and enlarge the reps stepper. Will rebuild and screenshot to verify.

instead of a notes button, let me swipe right on the screen to go to a notes screen -- just like on tiktok you can swipe right to go to someone's profile -- same idea. But the notes shouldn't just be a notes, it should be a note but it should run the agent, like maybe I write "this felt kinda weird, it hurt my back a little" and maybe the agent writes back "ok, try with 50% less weight and tell me how it feels" and it would adjust the next set's weight, or maybe the agent decides "ok, let's skip this one for a few sessions and ease back into it in two weeks" and makes notes and whatnot to change the program, etc. the log & next button we can remove since I would just swipe down to the next exercise when I'm done.
Build succeeded. Installing and capturing the new list sheet and Plank timer.
Build succeeded. Installing and walking through the new flow — runner controls, then swipe-right to Coach.
Confirmed the clean new runner: rate-effort glass capsule, big reps stepper, small Skip, and a vertical "Coach ‹" edge hint. Log & Next and Note buttons are gone.
Confirmed the effort dial morphs open to an RPE gradient track with a knob and amber Set button. Now testing the Coach swipe-right.
Coach screen is live: per-exercise title, terse seeded opener, Set button to return, and a glass input. Now testing a pain note to confirm the coach replies and edits the upcoming set.

Coach flow works end-to-end: user types a pain note, coach replies tersely ("Sharp or dull? Cut your next set to 50%."), applies a green diff (135 → 70 lb), and offers a Deload 2 weeks chip. Verified the runner's Set 2 now shows 70 lb.

All changes are live and verified on the simulator: swipe-right Coach screen, agent-driven plan editing, removed Log & Next and Note buttons, and the RPE effort dial. Built on a shared @Observable WorkoutSession so changes propagate live. Noted the Plank timer compiles but wasn't demoed this round, and flagged two minor polish items (effort dial should reset per set, list-sheet rows are uniformly blue).
